message:
fix: cursor visibility, multi-line input, scroll mode keybind cleanup

Cursor line black-on-black: the textarea cursor block uses Reverse(true)
on its own Style (not TextStyle/CursorLine), inverting whatever fg/bg the
cursor widget has. Explicitly set Cursor.Style.Foreground to inputFg so
the reversed block is visible against the theme background. Also set
Cursor.TextStyle for the blink-hidden state.

Multi-line input: remove MaxHeight=1 constraint; set MaxHeight=5 so the
input box grows up to 5 rows as the user types. vpHeight() computes
viewport height dynamically from m.input.Height() so the layout stays
correct as the input box grows or shrinks. refreshViewport() adjusts
viewport height on every render.

Send key changed from enter to ctrl+s; enter now inserts a newline in
the textarea. Removed ctrl+u as a scroll-mode entry key (it conflicts
with textarea delete-before-cursor); pgup alone enters scroll mode.

Update /help to document the new keybindings. Fix tests to use ctrl+s
for message submission.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -42,9 +42,11 @@ func slashCommandHelp() string {
 		"  /quit      Exit the TUI",
 		"",
 		"Keys:",
-		"  pgup / ctrl+u   Enter scroll mode (browse history)",
-		"  esc / i         Exit scroll mode",
-		"  tab             Expand/collapse most recent tool call",
+		"  enter       New line in input",
+		"  ctrl+s      Send message",
+		"  pgup        Enter scroll mode (browse history)",
+		"  esc / i     Exit scroll mode",
+		"  tab         Expand/collapse most recent tool call",
 	}, "\n")
 }
